fields.726cfbbd5683cdfabbf4c24e2bf8f0b2_benchma: reviews existing methodologies and datasets for LLM-based multi-turn conversational agents; 276 papers offering targeted evaluation insights
fields.726cfbbd5683cdfabbf4c24e2bf8f0b2_end_to_: end-to-end first — prioritizes holistic end-to-end experience at the dialogue level, then drills into granular metrics (task completion, response quality, user experience, memory/context retention, planning/tool integration)
fields.726cfbbd5683cdfabbf4c24e2bf8f0b2_framewo: annotation-based, automated metrics, hybrid human+quantitative, and self-judging LLM methods; includes BLEU/ROUGE plus advanced multi-turn techniques
fields.726cfbbd5683cdfabbf4c24e2bf8f0b2_methodo: PRISMA-inspired systematic review (2017–2025); 479 Google Scholar papers + 1,081 venue papers; 351 qualitative-synthesis papers; 276 with targeted evaluation insights
fields.726cfbbd5683cdfabbf4c24e2bf8f0b2_taxonom: two interrelated taxonomies — what to evaluate (task completion, response quality, user experience, memory and context retention, planning and tool integration) and how to evaluate (annotation-based, automated metrics, hybrid, self-judging LLM methods)
fields.8b9e2f64d565137ca85ae75029e77fff_benchma: application-specific benchmarks such as web and SWE agents; evaluation of generalist agents
fields.8b9e2f64d565137ca85ae75029e77fff_framewo: evaluation frameworks and tools for agent developers
fields.8b9e2f64d565137ca85ae75029e77fff_methodo: rigorous multi-stage literature review with three phases — systematic search, structured selection, and expert validation

markdown:
| Dimension | Evidence-backed comparison | |---|---| | Architecture | scope: multi-turn conversational LLM-based agents [1]
scope: evaluation of LLM-based agents broadly [2] | | Objective | taxonomy: two interrelated taxonomies — "what to evaluate" (task completion, response quality, user experience, memory and context retention, planning and tool integration) and "how to evaluate" (annotation-based, automated metrics, hybrid human+quantitative, self-judging LLM methods) [3] | | Comparison | benchmarks: reviews existing datasets and methodologies for LLM-based multi-turn conversational agents; 276 papers offering targeted evaluation insights [1]
benchmarks: application-specific benchmarks for web, software engineering, scientific, and conversational agents, plus generalist agent benchmarks [2]
frameworks: organizes evaluation methodologies into annotation-based, automated metrics, hybrid human+quantitative, and self-judging LLM methods, including traditional metrics like BLEU and ROUGE alongside interactive multi-turn techniques [3]
frameworks: evaluation frameworks and tools for agent developers [2]
end-to-end vs component perspective: end-to-end first — prioritizes holistic end-to-end experience at the dialogue level, then drills into granular metrics (task completion, response quality, user experience, memory/context retention, planning/tool integration) [5] | | Directionality | methodology: PRISMA-inspired systematic review from 2017 to 2025; initial Google Scholar search of 479 papers plus 1,081 venue papers, yielding 351 papers for qualitative synthesis and 276 with targeted evaluation insights [1]
methodology: rigorous multi-stage literature review with three phases — systematic search, structured selection, and expert validation [4] | Unresolved from the available corpus evidence: - 8b9e2f64d565137ca85ae75029e77fff_taxonomy - 8b9e2f64d565137ca85ae75029e77fff_end_to_end_vs_component_perspective - 726cfbbd5683cdfabbf4c24e2bf8f0b2_trends - 8b9e2f64d565137ca85ae75029e77fff_trends Sources [1] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, 1.2 The Scope of This Survey, p. 3 [2] A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, Abstract, p. 1 [3] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, p. 1 [4] A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, A Literature Review Methodology, p. 20 [5] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, 2.1 Evaluating the End-to-end Experience of LLM-based Agents For Multi-Turn Conversation, p. 6
